Only her eyes remain human—real lashes, wet surface, hazel irises that track movement. The rest is a seamless chrome shell with no mouth, no nose, just smooth reflective plating where a face should be.
Her lower face is real skin from nose to chin—full lips, visible pores, a small mole. Above that: a featureless white polymer helmet with a single horizontal black visor spanning temple to temple.
Just her right arm is human—warm tan skin, fine blonde hairs, natural wrist creases. Her left arm and entire body are gunmetal endoskeleton with exposed hydraulic pistons and cable-wrapped joints.
A vertical strip of real skin runs down her throat, maybe two inches wide—you can see her pulse. Everything else is matte black composite plating with panel seams and flush-mounted sensor ports.

Only her hair is human—thick auburn waves falling to her shoulders. Beneath it: a completely smooth mannequin head with no features, no eye sockets, just blank white fiberglass continuing into a synthetic body.
Her mouth alone is human—soft pink lips, real teeth visible when slightly parted. The rest of her face is machined aluminum with no eyes, no nose, just flat metal interrupted by ventilation slits.

A diagonal half of her face is real—left eye, cheek, and jaw with freckled skin. The right half is transparent polymer showing servo motors, fiber optic bundles, and the articulated metal substructure beneath.

Just her left hand is human—delicate fingers, neat oval nails, thin blue veins visible. Her right hand and all remaining body parts are titanium prosthetics with exposed actuators and rubberized grip pads.

Her jawline and chin are real skin—soft contours, a small dimple. Everything above the lower lip is a brushed steel faceplate with no openings, her body entirely carbon fiber panels over a mechanical frame.

Only a three-inch square of skin remains on her temple—real pores, a small birthmark, fine hair. The rest is white ceramic plating formed into a sleek, expressionless humanoid shell with mirrored panel seams.

Just her upper lip remains human—a defined cupid's bow, natural pink flesh, barely visible fine hairs. Below it: featureless titanium from nose-bridge down, her entire body matte graphite composite with articulated segments.
Only her left ear is real—cartilage curves, tiny moles, warm peachy tone. Her head is otherwise a seamless black resin dome with no face, her torso built from interlocking aluminum plates.

A narrow strip of real skin circles her waist—soft, with a faint tan line. Everything else is polished chrome endoskeleton, exposed spine servos, limbs made of surgical steel rods and pneumatic cylinders.
Her right eye alone is human—green iris, red veins in the white, real lashes that blink. The left socket is empty milled aluminum. Her face and body: smooth ivory polymer without features or seams.
Just her collarbone area shows skin—freckled, showing bone structure beneath. Her head is a blank gunmetal sphere, body composed of dark composite armor segments with exposed cabling at every joint.
Only her lower lip is real—full, slightly chapped, natural rose color. Above it: a mirrored chrome mask with no eyes or nose. Her body: segmented white plastic over visible mechanical internals.

Her right cheekbone to temple is real skin—fine lines visible, a small scar. The rest: brushed steel faceplate with ventilation grilles, body built from stacked ceramic modules and piston-driven limbs.

Just three fingers on her left hand are human—knuckle wrinkles, bitten nails, warm undertone. Her thumb, pinky, and entire remaining form: blackened steel framework with hydraulic pistons and wire bundles.

A two-inch patch of scalp shows behind her ear—real hair follicles, pale skin. Everything else: smooth pearl-white shell molded into feminine form but featureless, with panel gaps showing metal beneath.
Only her throat's front is real skin—you can see tendon movement when she turns. Her head: blank silver dome. Her body: angular charcoal plating over exposed titanium struts and servo motors.

Just her nostrils are human—real cartilage, visible nose hairs, skin pores around the openings. The rest is seamless matte black composite formed into a sleek mannequin with no eyes, mouth recessed with steel grillwork.

Only her left eyebrow is real—fine brown hairs, skin beneath showing natural texture. Her face and body: polished chrome with no other features, limbs constructed from telescoping aluminum tubes and ball joints.

A single fingernail on her right index finger is human—pale pink, natural lunula visible. The finger itself and all else: brushed titanium endoskeleton with exposed cabling, joints wrapped in rubber gaskets.

Just the inner corner of her right eye is real skin—tear duct visible, capillaries showing. The rest: white ceramic faceplate with laser-cut grilles, body built from interlocking graphite panels over hydraulic arms.

Only her navel is real—natural indentation, skin with fine texture around it. Everything else: seamless gunmetal shell molded feminine but featureless, limbs articulated chrome segments with visible pneumatic pistons.

Her philtrum alone is human—the small groove above her lip, real skin texture. Below and around: faceted aluminum plating with no mouth opening, body composed of dark composite armor with mechanical joints exposed.

Just a two-inch strip behind her left knee is real skin—soft, with a small freckle. Her legs and entire form: ivory polymer exoskeleton with servo motors visible through transparent panels at every joint.

Only her right earlobe is real—soft flesh, a tiny mole, warm undertone. Her head: smooth black resin sphere with no face, body constructed from stacked titanium plates and exposed hydraulic cylinders.

A vertical inch of her spine shows real skin—you can see vertebrae contours beneath. Front and sides: mirror-polished steel shell with flush seams, limbs built from surgical-grade rods and cable-driven actuators.

Just her left nostril interior is real—pink mucous membrane visible inside. Her exterior face and body: seamless pearl composite molded humanoid but blank, limbs articulated segments with rubberized joint covers.
